page: Amazon Web Services c7g.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture using Neoverse-V1 cores, this server features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz. It operates without hyperthreading, allocating one thread per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the c7g.large delivers moderate single-core capabilities but exhibits limitations under multi-threaded workloads. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, single-core CPU performance falls within the average tier, supported by solid single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, register in the weak category. Memory bandwidth tests show an average result of 52.81 GB/sec for large-block read operations, but write speeds and small-block cached reads are weak. Complex computational tasks, including ray tracing, image processing, and Passmark extended instruction tests, score in the poor range, reflecting the lack of advanced vector extensions like AVX or AMX.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7g.large offers an entry-level entry point into the Graviton7 family, trading off raw multi-threaded compute power and memory capacity for a lower resource footprint. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, text processing, and development workloads that benefit from ARM64 architecture alignment without requiring high memory density or local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, but weak multi-core scaling and poor performance in vector-heavy tasks like ray tracing due to the absence of AVX and AMX extensions. This instance represents a cost-efficient entry point for ARM-native development, lightweight single-threaded processing, basic web serving, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.large is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 2 Graviton vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.large is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 2 Graviton vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An entry-level ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores for lightweight single-threaded workloads and basic web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 processor
2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory
Average single-core performance with weak multi-core scaling
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and ARM development
